title: Reflected XSS via var_export() in wp_die() During Profile Save Error
description: When wp_update_user() returns a WP_Error during profile save, user-controlled profile data (display_name, nickname, first_name, last_name) is dumped via var_export() inside wp_die() wrapped in <pre> tags without HTML entity escaping. PHP's var_export() represents strings with single quotes but does NOT escape <, >, &, or double-quote characters, allowing injection of arbitrary HTML/JavaScript.
impact: Mostly self-XSS since the error page is returned to the user who submitted the profile update. Requires wp_update_user() to return a WP_Error, which is uncommon under normal conditions. Could theoretically be weaponized if an attacker can force the error condition.
remediation: Wrap the var_export() output in esc_html() before passing to wp_die(), or use wp_json_encode() instead of var_export() for debug output.
